analysis_result: UNABLE_TO_EVALUATE: BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE - the inferCNV + marker-based cell-typing pipeline required to distinguish neoplastic vs non-neoplastic nuclei from the 6 PNST snRNA-seq datasets was not executed (worker agent stalled due to rate limit). GSE207399_series_matrix.txt.gz does not contain per-barcode cell-type annotations, and no CNV inference (inferCNV) or Schwann/SCP marker-based scoring was performed. Paper reports 30,518 neoplastic and 12,847 non-neoplastic nuclei (~43,365 total).
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Paper: PMC10172395 (Fig 4a/b), reports snRNA-seq of 6 PNSTs with a total of ~43,365 nuclei partitioned into 30,518 neoplastic and 12,847 non-neoplastic populations.
   - Available data in data_dir: GSE207399_series_matrix.txt.gz (sample-level metadata only). Raw 10x matrices contain more nuclei than the final filtered set.
   - Per-barcode cell-type annotations (neoplastic vs. non-neoplastic labels) are NOT provided in the series matrix or other accessible supplementary files.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Target: 6 PNST snRNA-seq samples from Fig 4a/b.
   - Required classification: neoplastic vs. non-neoplastic nuclei per barcode.
   - Final analytic cohort in paper: ~43,365 nuclei after QC.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Neoplastic/non-neoplastic assignment in the paper was derived via inferCNV-based CNV inference plus Schwann/Schwann-cell-precursor (SCP) marker scoring.
   - No pre-computed cell-type labels are present in the accessible metadata files.

D. COMPUTATION (NOT EXECUTED)
   - Required pipeline: (1) ingest 10x matrices (scanpy load_10x); (2) QC filtering; (3) HVG/PCA/Leiden; (4) inferCNV (infercnvpy or R inferCNV) with a non-neoplastic reference population (immune/endothelial/fibroblast clusters); (5) marker-based scoring for Schwann (S100B, SOX10, PLP1) and SCP markers; (6) threshold CNV score + marker score to assign neoplastic vs. non-neoplastic.
   - This pipeline was NOT run: the worker agent stalled due to rate limit (BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE).

E. RESULT
   - No computed result is available. The model output is BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE.
   - Paper-reported result: 30,518 neoplastic + 12,847 non-neoplastic = 43,365 total.

F. CONCORDANCE ASSESSMENT
   - Cannot assess ±10% concordance because no model-derived value exists. By convention when the model cannot compute the statistic at all, this is treated as DISCREPANT / UNABLE_TO_EVALUATE rather than CONCORDANT.

G. ROOT CAUSE
   - 5:STATISTICAL_METHOD -- the required statistical/analytical method (inferCNV-based CNV inference + marker scoring) was not executed. The backend (worker agent) stalled on rate-limit before producing any cell-type assignments. Secondary contributor: per-barcode cell-type labels are not distributed in the deposited metadata, so the classification must be re-derived de novo, which is computationally expensive and requires both scanpy (or R inferCNV) and marker reference lists.
   - Proposed fix: re-run with (i) scanpy recipes `load_10x.py` -> `qc.py` -> `hvg_leiden.py` on each of the 6 PNST samples; (ii) `infercnv.py` with endothelial/immune clusters as CNV-neutral reference and a GENCODE GTF for gene positions; (iii) score Schwann/SCP markers (S100B, SOX10, PLP1, MPZ, NGFR) with `sc.tl.score_genes`; (iv) threshold CNV score at the reference distribution's 99th percentile AND require a positive Schwann/SCP score for 'neoplastic' assignment; (v) tabulate per-sample and aggregate counts. Alternatively, contact authors for per-barcode cell-type annotations to bypass the inferCNV step.
proposed_fix: Run the scanpy + infercnvpy pipeline: load_10x.py -> qc.py -> hvg_leiden.py on each of the 6 PNST samples; infercnv.py with immune/endothelial clusters as the CNV-neutral reference (requires a GENCODE GTF); score Schwann/SCP markers (S100B, SOX10, PLP1, MPZ, NGFR) with sc.tl.score_genes; assign 'neoplastic' when CNV score exceeds the reference 99th percentile AND the Schwann/SCP marker score is positive. Aggregate across the 6 PNSTs to tabulate neoplastic and non-neoplastic nucleus counts. Alternatively, request per-barcode cell-type annotations from the authors/GEO supplement.
